title: nothopanax simplex var. sinclairii
intro: Nothopanax simplex var. sinclairii, also known as the New Zealand matipo or poison tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Araliaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting wound healing. In traditional herbal medicine, Nothopanax simplex var. sinclairii is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and has also been used to make topical creams and ointments to aid in skin care and wound treatment.
